short_description: Payment processing system outage - transactions failing with HTTP 502
description: Multiple customers reporting failed payment transactions since 08:45 UTC. The payment processing pipeline is returning HTTP 502 Bad Gateway errors intermittently. Stripe webhook endpoint (/api/v1/webhooks/stripe) is rejecting incoming events with signature validation failures. Approximately 35% of checkout attempts are failing. Revenue impact estimated at $45K/hour. Engineering team has identified a recent deployment (v3.12.1) as the likely cause but rollback is blocked due to an associated database schema migration. Escalated to P1 by on-call engineer at 09:02 UTC.
business_impact: Direct revenue loss. Customer checkout flow broken for ~35% of transactions.
workaround: Manual payment reconciliation via Stripe dashboard. Customer service team processing refunds for stuck orders.

short_description: Critical security vulnerabilities detected in production - CVE-2026-31245, CVE-2026-28901
description: Automated vulnerability scanner (Snyk) flagged 3 critical CVEs in production microservices during scheduled weekly scan at 06:00 UTC. CVE-2026-31245 (log4j-core RCE) affects logging-service, audit-service, and notification-service. CVE-2026-28901 (Spring auth bypass) affects auth-gateway and user-service. CVE-2026-33102 (jackson deserialization) affects all Java-based services. CISO has mandated 48-hour remediation window. Proof-of-concept exploit code for CVE-2026-31245 is publicly available on GitHub. WAF rules deployed as temporary mitigation.
business_impact: Potential data breach risk. Regulatory compliance violation if not patched within mandated timeline.
workaround: WAF rules blocking known exploit patterns. Increased monitoring on affected services.

short_description: Database connection pool exhaustion on order-service causing cascading timeouts
description: The order-service PostgreSQL connection pool (HikariCP) is reaching maximum capacity (50 connections) during peak traffic hours (14:00-18:00 UTC), causing cascading timeout failures in downstream services. Connection leak suspected in the bulk order import feature introduced in Sprint 23. Active connections remain open after the import job completes. Pool metrics show 48/50 connections active with 23 threads in WAITING state. Temporary mitigation: restarting order-service pods every 2 hours via CronJob.
business_impact: Intermittent order processing delays during peak hours. 5-8% of API requests timing out.
workaround: Automated pod restart every 2 hours. Manual connection pool flush available via admin endpoint.

short_description: SSL/TLS certificate for api.taskpilot.io expiring in 48 hours
description: The wildcard SSL certificate for *.taskpilot.io is expiring on 2026-06-22T00:00:00Z. The automated renewal via cert-manager failed because the DNS-01 challenge solver is misconfigured after the recent migration from Route53 to CloudFlare DNS. cert-manager logs show: 'Failed to determine a]valid solver configuration: no configured challenge solvers can be used for this challenge.' All production API endpoints, the customer portal, and the developer documentation site will become inaccessible if the certificate expires. Requires manual certificate renewal or cert-manager reconfiguration.
business_impact: Complete service outage for all HTTPS endpoints if certificate expires. Affects all customers.
workaround: Manual certificate can be issued via certbot CLI as emergency measure.

short_description: Memory leak in notification microservice - OOM kills every 6 hours
description: The notification-service pods are being OOM-killed by Kubernetes approximately every 6 hours. Memory usage grows linearly from 256MB to the 1GB limit. Heap dump analysis reveals that Firebase Cloud Messaging client objects are not being garbage collected due to a circular reference in the connection pool wrapper. The leak was introduced in commit a3f7c2e (Sprint 23) when the FCM client was refactored to support connection multiplexing. Each notification batch creates a new client instance that is never closed. Approximately 4,200 orphaned client objects found in the latest heap dump.
business_impact: Push notifications delayed by 5-15 minutes during pod restart cycles. Some notifications lost during OOM events.
workaround: Increased pod memory limit to 1.5GB and added horizontal pod autoscaler to spin up replacement pods faster.

short_description: Third-party geocoding API (Mapbox) returning 503 errors intermittently
description: The Mapbox Geocoding API integration is returning HTTP 503 Service Unavailable for approximately 15% of requests since 2026-06-19T16:00:00Z. Affects the address validation feature in the checkout flow and the store locator functionality. Mapbox status page acknowledges degraded performance in their NA-East region. Our retry logic (3 attempts with exponential backoff) is partially mitigating the issue but adding 2-4 seconds of latency to affected requests. Need to implement fallback to Google Maps Geocoding API for resilience.
business_impact: Address validation in checkout delayed for ~15% of users. Store locator feature intermittently unavailable.
workaround: Retry logic handling most failures. Users can manually enter addresses to bypass validation.
